summary: Re-tested leaderboard fix from iter2. Verified GET /api/leaderboard now returns current_streak, longest_streak, medals, and weekly_points fields for ALL entries, including legacy users (Test Student, Test User have all four fields with default 0). The targeted test test_leaderboard_has_new_fields PASSED. Also re-ran full test_iteration2.py suite (20/20 PASSED) - no regressions.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix in seed_data() using update_many with $exists:false is idempotent and correct - applies defaults only to legacy docs missing the new fields.
Verified live response: legacy user 'Test Student' (id 3c9ccd83...) shows current_streak=0, longest_streak=0, medals=0, weekly_points=0 - backfill working as expected.
seed_data_creation: None - used existing seed + dynamic test users
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter2 leaderboard backfill fix verified. All 20 iteration2 tests pass. Legacy users now receive default values (0) for current_streak, longest_streak, medals, weekly_points via update_many in seed_data() on startup.
rca of the issue: Original bug: legacy users from iter1 did not have the new fields (current_streak, longest_streak, medals, weekly_points) in MongoDB, so /api/leaderboard responses for those entries omitted them. Fix: seed_data() now runs db.users.update_many({'current_streak': {'$exists': False}}, {'$set': {...defaults}}) on backend startup. Verified by inspecting raw API output: every entry (6 users) now contains all four fields.
